title: physostigma venenosum
intro: Physostigma venenosum, also known as the calabar bean or ouabain, is a plant native to West Africa that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include acting as a strong heart stimulant and a potent inhibitor of the heart's ability to pump blood, making it useful in treating conditions such as cardiac arrhythmias and high blood pressure. The calabar bean has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and certain types of cancer, as well as to induce vomiting and as a purgative. Herbal preparations of Physostigma venenosum can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and powdered beans, although caution should be exercised when using this plant due to its potential toxicity.
